---
# ============================================================================
# #equilibria — Article Frontmatter Contract
# ============================================================================
# Copy this template to tutorials/<section>/<slug>/index.qmd
# Replace all placeholders (ALL_CAPS or angle brackets).
# Every field documented below; required fields marked [REQUIRED].
# ============================================================================

# [REQUIRED] Article title — sentence case, specific
title: "ARTICLE_TITLE"

# [REQUIRED] 1-2 sentence description for listings and SEO
description: "ARTICLE_DESCRIPTION"

# [REQUIRED] Author
author: "Raban Heller"

# [REQUIRED] Publication date (ISO 8601)
date: 2026-XX-XX

# [OPTIONAL] Last modification date
date-modified: 2026-XX-XX

# [REQUIRED] Categories — first entry is the section slug, rest are tags
categories:
  - SECTION_SLUG
  - TAG_1
  - TAG_2
  - TAG_3

# [OPTIONAL] Keywords for SEO (not used in listings)
keywords: ["keyword1", "keyword2", "R"]

# [OPTIONAL] Labels for overview page filtering
labels: ["LABEL_1", "LABEL_2"]

# [REQUIRED] Tier: 1 (MVP), 2 (analytics-driven), or 3 (opportunistic)
tier: 1

# [REQUIRED] Bibliography path — adjust depth to match nesting
bibliography: ../../../references.bib

# [REQUIRED] VG Wort placeholder — format: TODO_VGWORT_<section>_<slug>
# Raban replaces with real pixel URL after registering at tom.vgwort.de
vgwort: "TODO_VGWORT_SECTION_SLUG"

# [REQUIRED] Thumbnail for listings (generate during article creation)
image: thumbnail.png
image-alt: "ALT_TEXT_DESCRIBING_THUMBNAIL"

# [REQUIRED] Citation metadata
citation:
  type: webpage
  url: https://r-heller.github.io/equilibria/tutorials/SECTION/SLUG/

# [REQUIRED] Content license
license: "CC BY 4.0"

# [REQUIRED] Draft status — set false when article is complete
draft: true

# [REQUIRED] Figure flags — CI checks these
has_static_fig: true
has_interactive_fig: true

# [OPTIONAL] Linked Shiny app slug (null if none)
has_shiny_app: null

# ============================================================================
# Section-specific extra fields (include only when applicable)
# ============================================================================

# --- For ethics-and-game-theory / ethics-applications articles ---
# framework: "consequentialism"  # or deontology, contractualism, etc.

# --- For ai-ml-foundations-and-applications articles ---
# ai_ml:
#   technique_class: "supervised"  # supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement, etc.
#   interpretability: "open-box"   # open-box, closed-box, augmented
#   ai_type: "narrow"              # narrow, general, frontier
#   benchmark_referenced: null     # benchmark name or null

# --- For public-apis-and-datasets articles ---
# api_or_dataset:
#   name: "API_NAME"
#   type: "REST"                   # REST, GraphQL, R-package, dataset
#   access: "free-no-auth"         # free-no-auth, free-with-key, academic, etc.
#   license: "CC-BY"
#   r_package: "package_name"
#   endpoint_or_url: "https://api.example.com/v1/"
#   domain: "economics"
#   last_verified: 2026-XX-XX
---

<!-- ====================================================================== -->
<!-- ARTICLE BODY — minimum 2500 prose characters (excluding YAML/code/refs) -->
<!-- ====================================================================== -->

{{< include ../../../R/_common.R >}}

## Introduction & motivation

<!-- 400–600 chars: concrete problem, why it matters, real example. -->

INTRODUCTION_TEXT


## Mathematical formulation

<!-- 500–800 chars + LaTeX: formal definitions, named theorem references. -->

MATH_TEXT

$$
LATEX_EQUATION
$$


## R implementation

<!-- 200–400 chars + working code chunk. -->

IMPLEMENTATION_TEXT

```{r}
#| label: CHUNK_LABEL
#| fig-cap: "FIGURE_CAPTION"
#| dev: [png, pdf]
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| dpi: 300

source(here::here("R", "_common.R"))

# --- Working R code (30–60 lines, well-commented) ---
```


## Static publication-ready figure

<!-- 200 chars + chunk producing PDF + PNG @ 300dpi via save_pub_fig(). -->

STATIC_FIG_TEXT

```{r}
#| label: fig-SLUG-static
#| fig-cap: "Figure 1. WHAT_THE_FIGURE_SHOWS. METHOD. INTERPRETATION. Data: SOURCE (LICENSE)."
#| dev: [png, pdf]
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| dpi: 300

# p <- ggplot(...) + theme_publication() + scale_colour_okabe_ito()
# save_pub_fig(p, "figures/SLUG-static")
# p
```


## Interactive figure

<!-- 200 chars + chunk producing plotly or htmlwidget output. -->

INTERACTIVE_FIG_TEXT

```{r}
#| label: fig-SLUG-interactive

# to_plotly_pub(p, tooltip = c("text"))
```


## Simulation or extension

<!-- 300–500 chars + code, often Python via reticulate. -->

SIMULATION_TEXT

```{r}
#| label: simulation-SLUG

# Additional analysis, simulation, or Python via reticulate
```


## Interpretation

<!-- 400–600 chars: what the result tells us, limitations, sensitivity. -->

INTERPRETATION_TEXT


<!-- ====================================================================== -->
<!-- OPTIONAL: Linked Shiny app (only if has_shiny_app != null)             -->
<!-- ====================================================================== -->

<!-- Uncomment and fill if this article has a paired Shiny app:

## Linked Shiny app

::: {.app-embed}
<iframe src="https://r-heller.shinyapps.io/APP_SLUG/" width="100%" height="600" frameborder="0"></iframe>

[Open app in new tab](https://r-heller.shinyapps.io/APP_SLUG/) · [View source on GitHub](https://github.com/r-heller/equilibria/tree/main/shiny-apps/NN-APP_SLUG/)
:::

-->


## Extensions & related tutorials

<!-- 200–400 chars + 3–5 internal crosslinks. -->

EXTENSIONS_TEXT

- [Related tutorial 1](../../SECTION/SLUG/)
- [Related tutorial 2](../../SECTION/SLUG/)
- [Related tutorial 3](../../SECTION/SLUG/)


## References

::: {#refs}
:::
